old_claim.note: Organized Military Formations provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No redundant general-formation edge remains because the retained musketry-formation prerequisite already carries organized rank drill into this mixed tactic.

source_shape.source_locator: Object description documents the specific ranked interaction of pikemen and musketeers rather than a separate causal role for generic ancient formations.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The documented tactic consists of pikemen protecting flanking musketeers as those firearm troops fire and reload.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Keeping the specific musketry path preserves organized drill without duplicating its remote generic ancestor on the mixed formation.

ontology_before: Pike and shot had both a specific musketry-formation dependency and its generic organized-formation ancestor as parallel prerequisites.
ontology_after: The mixed tactic inherits organization through the specific musketry-formation path.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: pike_and_shot_formations no longer directly depends on organized_military_formations.
would_reject_if: The musketry prerequisite ceased to encode organized infantry ranks or a distinct direct organization mechanism were documented.
short_reason: The generic formation edge duplicated the retained specific path.
